titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L663
GA L663
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Verse marker for 1.18 removed from M5D4 by David Parker.
Verse markers for 14.22 and 14.24 added in M9D11b by David Parker.
Redundant verse marker in 12.32 F286v removed by Rachel Kevern.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 W1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-A number: L 663 (Athos, Εsphigmenou 20)Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1rJn 8.3-11 occurs at Menol., Οct. 8Τranscribed by: RLMΤranscription begun: 30.05.02 Τranscription finished: 07.11.02Τransference to Collate by: RLMΤransference to Collate begun: 30.05.02Τransference to Collate finished: 07.11.02File date and time:Ρlaced in Level1: RLMLevel1 file date and time: 31.10.02Ρlaced in Level2: read a second time and compared with the collation of Johannes Karavidopoulos. Ηoly Week and Menologion read in MuensterΡlaced in Level2 by: RLMLevel2 file date and time: 15.11.02Τhese files were compared and differences resolved by:Ρlaced in Level3:Ρlaced in Level3 by:Final transcription file date and time: Page layout added by: acmStart date: 29.8.13 End date: 3.9.13 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks: U 1> Corresponds with M 2 D 8 (John 8, 3-11) on F246r.U 2> Corresponds with M 4 D 22 (John 10, 22-30) on F253v.U 3> Corresponds with M 5 D 2 (John 3, 1-12) on F261r.U 4> Corresponds with M 9 D 11a (John 15, 9-16) on F275r.U 5> Corresponds with M 9 D 11b (John 14, 15-27) on F275v.U 6> Corresponds with M 9 D 26 (John 14, 21-24) on F276v.U 7> Corresponds with M 12 D 12 (John 12, 35-36, 44-50) on F286v.U 8> Corresponds with M 12 D 31 (John 14, 12-14; 15, 8-11) on F291v.
1. συναξαριον i: πασχα to πεντηκοστη - ευαγγελια κατα ιωαννην - S1 W1 D1 - S1 W8 D1 Easter to Pentecost

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday
John 6,35-39 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D6)
John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman

ab.note: The image of this folio is too poor to read.
The image of this folio is too poor to read.

div.note: The lection heading and the beginning of this lection are too poor to read.
John 14,1-11a (Scrivener John 14,1-10; Gale John 14,1-11, Wake John 14,1-12)
John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)
John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτουργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)
The heading instructs the user to read this pericope on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.
Our lectionary index has Matthew 3. 1-6 on this day.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.

The position of this lection suggests that it is read on May 26th but our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day, nor does this lection correspond to the correct Saint's Day in the Orthodox calendar.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.
Our lectionary index has Luke 10. 38-42; 11. 27-28 on this day.
